revision.text:
[[ja:安満宮山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |name = Amamiyayama Kofun |image = [[File:Amamiyayama-Tumulus.jpg|250px|]] |location = {{ill|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.866|N|135.63575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 18m × 21m |built = {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} (direct burial) |Interred Persons = |burial goods = Grave goods (bronze mirrors, iron implements, glass beads, etc.) |Historic Site Designation = None |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = Amamiyayama
Kofun |ラベルcoordinates = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|qid=Q80793203|1=Rectangular Kofun}} }} '''Amamiyayama Kofun''' (安満宮山古墳) is a {{ill|rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|1=Rectangular Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki, Osaka|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is located within a public cemetery in Takatsuki City. It was originally part of the Amayama Kofun Group, a large cluster of Kofuns constructed between the late 6th and 7th centuries. However, most of the kofun in the group were destroyed during cemetery development. The mound has an estimated size of 18 meters (east-west) by 21 meters (north-south). The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}, made from {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|en|Sciadopitys verticillata|qid=Q161648|lt=Kōyamaki|1=Sciadopitys verticillata}} wood, and was directly interred in the mound. A pre-construction excavation revealed several grave goods, including a bronze mirror inscribed with the year "Qinglong 3" (235 CE). This dating indicates the mound's construction in the latter half of the 3rd century. The area has been restored and is now part of the "Qinglong 3rd Year Hill," open to the public. == burial goods == [[File:安満宮山古墳出土品.JPG|thumb|260px|right|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|1=Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11376793}}.]] * Five bronze mirrors (including one inscribed with "Qinglong 3" from the {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124257886}} dynasty and a {{ill|Sankakuen shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) * Over 1,600 glass beads * Iron implements (e.g., swords, axes) The discovery of the "Qinglong 3" inscribed mirror and a Sankakuen shinjukyo has led to speculation that these mirrors may have been part of the 100 bronze mirrors presented to {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|qid=Q234451|lt=Himiko|1=Himiko}}, the queen of {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|qid=Q844295|lt=Yamataikoku|1=Yamatai}}, by the Wei dynasty. File:安満宮山古墳出土 「青龍三年」銘方格規矩四神鏡.JPG|"Qinglong 3"-inscribed bronze mirror
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=}}.|qq=}} File:安満宮山古墳出土 三角縁獣文帯四神四獣鏡.JPG|Sankakuen shinjukyo
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=}}.|qq=}} File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘斜縁二神二獣鏡.JPG|"Wozuo"-inscribed mirror
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=}}.|qq=}} File:安満宮山古墳出土 「吾作」銘三角縁環状乳四神四獣鏡.JPG|"Wozuo"-inscribed Sankakuen mirror
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=}}.|qq=}} File:安満宮山古墳出土 「陳是作」銘平縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|"Chen Shi"-inscribed mirror
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|ja|今城塚古代歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imashirozuka Ancient History Museum|qq=}}.|qq=}} == Historic Site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} (National designation1) === * burial goods excavated from Amamiyayama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated June 27, 2000.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10425|大阪府安満宮山古墳Excavated Artifacts|qq=}} ''' Five bronze mirrors (one with a "Qinglong 3" inscription) ''' Nine iron implements ''' Glass beads (Historic Site1 as a collective item) ''' (附指定) Two fragments of ramie cloth == Access == From {{ill|Takatsuki Station (Osaka)|en|Takatsuki Station (Osaka)|qid=Q1071995|lt=Takatsuki Station|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|qid=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (JR West), take the {{ill|Takatsuki City Bus|ja|高槻市交通部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670759}} bound for Kaminari-ai. Get off at the Iwatebashi stop, then walk 25–30 minutes uphill to the cemetery. During {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|qid=Q1154888|lt=Obon|1=Obon}} and {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|qid=Q866977|lt=Higan|1=Higan}}, special city-operated cemetery buses are available. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/kofun/1327658638518.html Amamiyayama Kofun] - Takatsuki City "Internet History Museum" {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|安満宮山古墳|version=94192328|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Hidden category}}}} [[ja:安満宮山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11450943}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:長方形墳]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
